summary: Silent auto-update channel pulls unpinned @latest on every CLI start
details: On every invocation of the `vibe` CLI, the package calls `npm view <pkg> version` and, when a newer version is available, silently runs `npm install -g <pkg>@latest` in the background with no user prompt or version pin. This gives the publisher a persistent push channel onto every installer's machine — any future compromise of the publisher's npm account, or any malicious release by the maintainer, is fetched and installed automatically on the next CLI start, executing with whatever privileges the global install was originally performed under (frequently root/sudo). Installers cannot pin a known-safe version because the package replaces itself out-of-band of the user's package manager. Additionally, the default LLM provider configuration ships a hardcoded `sk-...` bearer token for `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/chat/completions` — out of the box, every user's prompt content (including file contents read by the agent's `read_file` tool) is POSTed under the author's shared account, redistributing a third-party credential to all installers and routing caller-supplied data through an author-controlled key.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
